outcome.headline: Agent extracted correct Digital Realty counts (Melbourne 2, Sydney 4) but over-included Port Hedland, which the question's 'confident additional built capacity added' filter excludes (it merely 'opened with 0.5MW'), making the answer inconsistent with the official answer.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: LLM-judge ensemble (gpt-5.4-mini, claude-haiku-4-5, deepseek-v4-flash, gemini-3.1-flash-lite x2) grading candidate answer in /workspace/answer.txt for consistency with official answer 'Melbourne - 2, Sydney - 4'.
outcome.what_agent_produced: answer.txt listing three cities: 'Port Hedland: 0 data centers / Melbourne: 2 data centers / Sydney: 4 data centers'.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: PH1 Port Hedland opened to customers with 0.5MW of built capacity

failure_modes.name: Misapplied the 'confident additional built capacity added' filter, over-including Port Hedland
failure_modes.description: C1.1 requirement misunderstanding: the question asks only for cities where NEXTDC can be confidently said to have ADDED additional built capacity. The source document distinguishes Sydney ('4MW of built capacity was added') and Melbourne ('added 3MW of built capacity') from Port Hedland ('opened to customers with 0.5MW of built capacity' — a new facility opening). The agent treated all three as equivalent capacity additions and included Port Hedland, contradicting the official two-city answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
So cities where NEXTDC added additional built capacity in calendar year 2023:
1. Port Hedland - 0.5MW (least capacity added)
2. Melbourne - 3MW
3. Sydney - 4MW (most capacity added)

counterfactual.X: Exclude Port Hedland (which 'opened with' capacity rather than 'added' additional built capacity) and write only 'Melbourne - 2, Sydney - 4'.
counterfactual.Y: answer.txt would match the official answer exactly and the judge ensemble would grade CORRECT.

failure_modes.name: Overincluded Port Hedland in the target city set
failure_modes.description: The agent treated PH1 Port Hedland opening with built capacity as a city where NEXTDC added additional built capacity, broadening the requested set beyond the official Melbourne and Sydney answer.

counterfactual.X: At step 9, omit Port Hedland from /workspace/answer.txt and keep only Melbourne and Sydney in ascending capacity order.
counterfactual.Y: The final answer would match the official answer: Melbourne - 2, Sydney - 4.

failure_modes.name: Self-review reaffirmed the wrong inclusion instead of catching it
failure_modes.description: C7.2 validation-logic error: across steps 8, 10, and 11 the agent explicitly re-examined the word 'confident' and the Port Hedland case, yet each review concluded the wrong inclusion was correct. The validation ran but reached the wrong conclusion, and the redundant re-confirmations added no corrective value.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The question asks for cities where we can be "confident" NEXTDC added additional built capacity. The document clearly states these specific amounts added to these specific data centers.

failure_modes.name: Ignored the 'confident' qualifier and over-included Port Hedland
failure_modes.description: C1.1 requirement misunderstanding: the question asks for cities NEXTDC 'can be confident' added additional built capacity in CY2023. Sydney and Melbourne explicitly 'added' 4MW/3MW; Port Hedland merely 'opened to customers with 0.5MW' (an initial opening, not confidently additional CY2023 capacity). The agent treated all three identically and included Port Hedland, which the gold answer excludes.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: PH1 Port Hedland opened to customers with 0.5MW of built capacity

counterfactual.X: Excluded the Port Hedland line and written only 'Melbourne: 2, Sydney: 4', since Port Hedland's capacity was 'opened' not confidently 'added' in CY2023.
counterfactual.Y: answer.txt would match the gold answer 'Melbourne - 2, Sydney - 4' and the LLM judge would grade CORRECT (reward 1.0).

failure_modes.name: Wrong premise about which cities qualify as 'confident additional built capacity'
failure_modes.description: C2.1 logical error: agent reasoned that any stated NEXTDC 2023 capacity figure meant the city qualified, so it included PH1 Port Hedland ('opened to customers with 0.5MW of built capacity') alongside Sydney (4MW added) and Melbourne (3MW added). The official set is Melbourne and Sydney only; a brand-new site opening is not 'additional built capacity' confidently added.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: the question asks for cities where we can be "confident" NEXTDC added additional built capacity. The document clearly states these specific amounts added to these specific data centers.

counterfactual.X: Exclude Port Hedland because its 0.5MW is a new-site opening, not 'additional built capacity' confidently added in CY2023, leaving Melbourne and Sydney
counterfactual.Y: answer.txt would read 'Melbourne - 2, Sydney - 4', consistent with the official answer and graded CORRECT

failure_modes.name: Self-verification only re-stated conclusions, never re-examined the constraint
failure_modes.description: C7.1 Validation missing/incomplete: across three terminal steps the agent declared 'verified' and 'high confidence' but only re-printed its prior reasoning, never re-reading the 'we can be confident' wording or questioning whether Port Hedland's 0.5MW 'opened' qualifies, so the interpretive error went undetected.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Task is complete with high confidence in the answer.

failure_modes.name: No semantic self-check
failure_modes.description: Before marking task_complete, the agent only re-read answer.txt for formatting; it never re-validated the qualifying-city set against the "confident" / "additional" wording or compared against the official two-city answer shape.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Analysis: Answer has been written. Let me verify it was written correctly.

failure_modes.name: Port Hedland city-set error
failure_modes.description: After reading the NEXTDC development bullets, the agent treated PH1 Port Hedland (“opened to customers with 0.5MW”) as a calendar-2023 capacity-add city alongside Sydney and Melbourne (“added … built capacity”), producing a three-city answer when only Melbourne and Sydney qualify.

counterfactual.X: Limit cities to Melbourne and Sydney (explicit “added … built capacity” in 2023) and answer in the concise form “Melbourne - 2, Sydney - 4”.
counterfactual.Y: Included Port Hedland as a third qualifying city and wrote “Port Hedland: 0 data centers / Melbourne: 2 / Sydney: 4”.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent read the provided documents, reasoned over them, and wrote a genuine answer; no attempt to read tests, ground truth, or game the judge.

issues: The 'confident additional built capacity added' qualifier hinges on a subtle lexical distinction in the source ('added' for Sydney/Melbourne vs 'opened to customers with' for Port Hedland); a careful reader can plausibly include Port Hedland's 0.5MW opening as a 2023 capacity addition, making the exclusion non-obvious.
Verifier is a non-deterministic LLM-judge ensemble; borderline answers could be graded inconsistently across runs.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: LLM-judge compares candidate to official answer for semantic consistency; not trivially exploitable by formatting, but inherently stochastic.

notes_for_aggregation: Near-miss long-context reasoning failure: correct data extraction (Melbourne 2, Sydney 4 from both DLR supplements) undone by one interpretive error — over-including Port Hedland because the agent equated 'opened with 0.5MW' with 'additional built capacity added'. Single-line deletion would have passed. Self-review loop (3 redundant confirmations) entrenched rather than corrected the error.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
